content.text:
<user_query>
There is a problem with Storybook—build, runtime, or configuration—for the UILIP package. No components are rendering; I only see an endless spinning loading icon on every story page. @portugal/packages/ui-lib/.storybook @portugal/packages/ui-lib/vite.config.ts @portugal/packages/ui-lib/src/components/plot-profile-dashboard-widget/PlotProfileDashboardWidget.stories.tsx 

Please compare the setup to the reference project that also runs several Storybook instances next to each other, all dependent on the central UI package.@Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib 

$ storybook dev -p 6007

Comparing Storybook and Vite configs between portugal's ui-lib and the reference project to find why stories show an endless spinner.

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: Procertus strips the root `vite.config.ts` `@` alias in `viteFinal` because it points at `ui-lib/src` and hijacks `@/` imports inside `packages/ui`, breaking resolution and React context. Portugal's `ui-lib` omits that strip. Applying the Procertus fix and aligning docgen `include` paths.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Build Storybook static bundle to verify config

content.text:

[REDACTED]

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Reverting the ineffective `tsconfigPath` change and restoring the Procertus-style `include` globs. The docgen plugin still doesn't pick up the merged program; the critical fix is stripping the `@` alias.

[REDACTED]

## Cause

Storybook merges `packages/ui-lib/vite.config.ts`, which defines:

```12:15:/Users/psoudan/workspace/11-personal/portugal/packages/ui-lib/vite.config.ts
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      "@": path.resolve(rootDir, "./src"),
```

That `@` alias always points at **ui-lib** `src/`. Shadcn-style code in **`packages/ui`** uses `@/…` for **`packages/ui/src`**. With that alias left in place, those imports were resolved to the wrong tree (ui-lib), which breaks primitives and **shared React context** (same class of bug Procertus documents in their `main.ts`). That typically shows up as broken hooks/context and a preview that never settles—often an endless spinner.

## What we changed (aligned with Procertus `ui-lib`)

In `packages/ui-lib/.storybook/main.ts`:

1. **`viteFinal` — strip the `@` alias** before merging plugins, so only `workspaceUiAliasPlugin` maps `@/` based on the **importer** (ui vs ui-lib). This matches `Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/.storybook/main.ts` (lines 35–48 there).

2. **`resolve.dedupe`** — add `"@portugal/ui"` next to `react` / `react-dom`, mirroring Procertus’s `dedupe` of `@procertus-ui/ui`.

3. **Stories** — drop the extra `../ui/src/showcase/**` glob (nothing on disk), which removes the “No story files found” noise.

4. **Docgen `include`** — keep absolute globs via `path.join(packageRoot, …)` and `path.join(uiPackageRoot, …)` like Procertus (your earlier relative `../ui/...` strings were weaker for tooling).

`vite.config.ts` itself is unchanged on purpose: the comment in `main.ts` already says not to merge the full app Vite config into Storybook; the fix belongs in Storybook’s `viteFinal`.

## Docgen warnings

You may still see “Skipping docgen … not included in the active TypeScript project” for files under `packages/ui`. That comes from `@joshwooding/vite-plugin-react-docgen-typescript` and **does not block** the preview; Procertus uses the same `include` pattern. Fixing it fully would mean a dedicated docgen/tsconfig strategy if you want zero warnings.

After pulling these changes, run `bun run storybook` from `packages/ui-lib` again—the canvas should render instead of spinning indefinitely.

[REDACTED]

Show main.ts content in latest feature commit
Full git status including all untracked

content.text:
Storybook fix is already in `77f1b33` (working tree clean). Adding `storybook-static` to `.gitignore` and committing — it's untracked build output from our `build-storybook` run; we won't commit the artifact.

[REDACTED]

input.command:
cd /Users/psoudan/workspace/11-personal/portugal && git add .gitignore && git status && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: ignore Storybook static export directory

`storybook build` writes `storybook-static/` under packages (for example
ui-lib). Treat it like other build output so it is not committed by mistake.
EOF
)"

content.text:
Here is what was true in the repo and what was committed.

### Already on `main`
The Storybook fix for `packages/ui-lib` (strip `@` alias in `viteFinal`, dedupe `@portugal/ui`, docgen `include`, drop empty showcase glob) is **already in history** — it matches `77f1b33` (`feat(frontend-web): expand plot workspace with dashboards and panels`). There was **no pending diff** for that file.

### New commit (this request)
The only leftover from the session was an untracked **`packages/ui-lib/storybook-static/`** tree from `build-storybook`. Per the commit skill, that build output should not be committed, so **`storybook-static` was added to the root `.gitignore`** and committed:

**`3718741`** — `chore: ignore Storybook static export directory`  
Body explains that `storybook build` writes `storybook-static/` and it should be treated like other build artifacts.

Your branch is now **4 commits** ahead of `origin/main` (the new one on top of the previous 3).

[REDACTED]
